dependencies.relationship: Requires fully implemented workspace infrastructure (table, handlers, routes, permissions, CLI, auth) from spec 01_workspaces

task_groups.title: Write tests: workspace schema DDL and new metadata fields (REQ-1)

subtasks.title: Write DDL schema test for display_name and description columns
details: Create test file internal/workspace/schema_test.go (or similar).
Test executes the workspaces CREATE TABLE DDL and runs PRAGMA table_info('workspaces').
Assert columns 'display_name' (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '') and 'description' (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '') are present.
Use go test table-driven style; test must compile and fail before implementation.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: display_name defaults to slug when omitted or cleared at creation
details: POST /api/v1/workspaces without display_name → assert response display_name == slug.
POST with display_name: null and display_name: '' → assert response display_name == slug (normalization).
Confirm no workspace row created on 400 error path.
Use httptest.NewServer or similar Go test infrastructure.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: description defaults to empty string when omitted or null at creation
details: POST /api/v1/workspaces without description → assert response description == ''.
POST with description: null → assert response description == ''.
Confirm backward-compatible creation still succeeds.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: display_name and description non-null across all response operations
details: For create, list, GET, PATCH, archive, reactivate: assert display_name and description are present non-null strings.
Assert all required fields (slug, git_url, branch, display_name, description, owner_id, org_id, status, created_at, updated_at) present in every response.
Table-driven test iterating over operation types.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: provided display_name and description stored and returned at creation
details: POST with non-empty display_name (<=128 chars) → assert response display_name equals input.
POST with non-empty description (<=1024 chars) → assert response description equals input.
POST with both fields populated → assert both values in response.
POST without either field → assert server-side defaults (slug / '').

subtasks.title: Write edge-case tests: field length validation at creation
details: POST with display_name of 129 chars → expect HTTP 400 + JSON {error: string}, no DB row created.
POST with description of 1025 chars → expect HTTP 400 + JSON {error: string}, no DB row created.
Boundary tests: exactly 128 chars and exactly 1024 chars must succeed (HTTP 201).

checks: All test files in this group compile: go build ./...
Tests fail as expected (red) before implementation: go test ./internal/workspace/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSpec03_Group1'
No linter warnings introduced: go vet ./...
Test IDs TS-03-1 through TS-03-6, TS-03-26 through TS-03-29, TS-03-E1 through TS-03-E4 are all present and named correctly

task_groups.title: Write tests: permission scope registration and access control matrix (REQ-2, REQ-3)

subtasks.title: Write unit tests: MountHandlers registers workspaces:write and workspaces:delete
details: Call MountHandlers in a test context and capture registered apikit.Permission entries.
Assert 'workspaces:write' and 'workspaces:delete' are present in the registry.
Assert 'workspaces:read' and 'workspaces:create' are still present and unchanged.
Verify existing PATs (read/create) still receive HTTP 200 for their permitted operations.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: workspaces:write implies read; workspaces:delete does not
details: PAT with workspaces:write: GET /api/v1/workspaces and GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug → HTTP 200.
PAT with only workspaces:delete: GET /api/v1/workspaces → HTTP 404 (anti-enumeration).
PAT with only workspaces:delete: GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug → HTTP 404.
Response JSON must contain {error: string} on 404 paths.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: admin token access matrix (update, archive, reactivate, delete)
details: Admin token: PATCH, archive, reactivate, DELETE on any workspace → HTTP 200 for each.
Admin token: POST /api/v1/workspaces → HTTP 403 with {error: string}.
Use a workspace owned by a different user to confirm cross-user admin access.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: owner API key access matrix
details: Workspace owner API key: PATCH, archive, reactivate, DELETE own workspace → HTTP 200 each.
Sequence: PATCH → archive → reactivate → archive → DELETE.
Confirm final DELETE removes the row from workspaces table.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: workspaces:write PAT access and workspaces:delete PAT access
details: PAT workspaces:write: PATCH, archive, reactivate → HTTP 200 each.
PAT workspaces:write: DELETE → HTTP 404 (anti-enumeration, no delete allowed).
PAT workspaces:delete on archived workspace: DELETE → HTTP 200.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: read/create PAT denied on mutation endpoints; anti-enumeration on unowned workspaces
details: PAT workspaces:read and workspaces:create: PATCH, archive, reactivate, DELETE → HTTP 404 each.
Non-owner user (u2) attempts GET, PATCH, archive, DELETE on workspace owned by u1 → HTTP 404 each.
Confirm {error: string} in all 404 responses.

checks: All test files in this group compile: go build ./...
Tests fail as expected (red) before implementation: go test ./internal/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSpec03_Group2'
No linter warnings introduced: go vet ./...
Test IDs TS-03-7 through TS-03-18, TS-03-E5, TS-03-E6 are all present and named correctly

task_groups.title: Write tests: workspace update endpoint PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug (REQ-4)

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: valid PATCH updates fields and sets updated_at
details: PATCH with {description: 'new'} → HTTP 200, description updated, updated_at strictly greater than initial.
PATCH with only display_name → description and org_id unchanged in DB.
All workspace fields present in HTTP 200 response.
Use RFC 3339 timestamp parsing to compare updated_at values.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: normalization of null/empty display_name and description in PATCH
details: PATCH with display_name: null → response display_name == slug.
PATCH with display_name: '' → response display_name == slug.
PATCH with description: null → response description == ''.
PATCH with description: '' → response description == ''.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: org_id update and removal in PATCH
details: PATCH with org_id: null → response org_id is null, DB row org_id = NULL.
PATCH with valid org_id (owner is member) → response org_id equals new value, HTTP 200.
Verify DB state directly after each operation.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: immutable field rejection in PATCH
details: PATCH bodies including slug, git_url, branch, or owner_id each return HTTP 400 + {error: string}.
Table-driven test over {slug, git_url, branch, owner_id} → assert 400 for each.
Confirm no mutation to workspaces table on rejection.

subtasks.title: Write edge-case tests: PATCH error conditions (empty body, archived, field length, org membership, not found, anti-enumeration)
details: Empty body {} → HTTP 400, updated_at unchanged in DB.
Archived workspace → HTTP 400, no changes written.
display_name > 128 chars → HTTP 400, display_name unchanged in DB.
description > 1024 chars → HTTP 400, description unchanged in DB.
Owner not member of new org → HTTP 403, org_id unchanged.
Non-existent slug → HTTP 404.
Existing workspace, non-owner non-admin → HTTP 404 (anti-enumeration).

subtasks.title: Write edge-case test: org membership service timeout returns HTTP 500 with no partial write
details: Configure mock org membership service to return error/timeout.
PATCH with non-null org_id → HTTP 500 + {error: string}.
Assert org_id in workspaces table remains NULL (no partial write).
Use context timeout or mock error injection.

checks: All test files in this group compile: go build ./...
Tests fail as expected (red) before implementation: go test ./internal/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSpec03_Group3'
No linter warnings introduced: go vet ./...
Test IDs TS-03-19 through TS-03-25, TS-03-E7 through TS-03-E14 are all present and named correctly

task_groups.title: Write tests: CLI commands afc workspace update and afc workspace create flags (REQ-7, REQ-8)

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: afc workspace update success path and PATCH body construction
details: Run afc workspace update <slug> --display-name 'CLI Updated' → exit 0, stdout valid JSON with display_name='CLI Updated'.
--clear-display-name, --clear-description, --clear-org → captured PATCH body has null values for corresponding fields.
Only --display-name provided → PATCH body contains only display_name key; description and org_id absent.
Use subprocess execution and HTTP mock server to capture request bodies.

subtasks.title: Write edge-case tests: afc workspace update error handling
details: No flags provided → exit 1, usage hint on stderr, zero HTTP requests made.
API returns non-2xx → exit 1, API error message on stderr, stdout empty.
Network timeout → exit 1 within configured timeout, error on stderr, no hang.
API returns HTTP 200 with malformed JSON → exit 1, parse error description on stderr.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests: afc workspace create --display-name and --description flags
details: afc workspace create --slug ws --git-url url --display-name 'CLI Created' --description 'Created via CLI' → exit 0, stdout JSON with both values.
afc workspace create without --display-name and --description → exit 0, stdout JSON with slug as display_name and '' as description.
Confirm backward compatibility: existing flags unchanged.

checks: All CLI test files compile: go build ./...
Tests fail as expected (red) before implementation: go test ./internal/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSpec03_Group4'
No linter warnings introduced: go vet ./...
Test IDs TS-03-30 through TS-03-34, TS-03-E15 through TS-03-E18 are all present

task_groups.title: Write tests: property-based invariants (PROP-1 through PROP-7)

subtasks.title: Write property tests: display_name and description never null in any response
details: Use go-fuzz or rapid (Go property test library) to generate arbitrary slug and display_name/description values.
For each generated input: create workspace, perform list/get/update/archive/reactivate, assert display_name is non-null non-empty string and description is non-null string.
Cover null and empty-string inputs to verify normalization invariant.

subtasks.title: Write property tests: partial update does not clobber omitted fields; updated_at advances
details: Generate arbitrary subsets of {display_name, description, org_id} for PATCH body.
Record pre-PATCH DB state; apply PATCH; assert omitted fields unchanged in DB.
Assert updated_at in response strictly greater than pre-PATCH updated_at (RFC 3339 comparison).

subtasks.title: Write property tests: immutable fields unchanged; workspaces:delete no read; existing scopes stable
details: For any PATCH (valid or invalid): assert slug, git_url, branch, owner_id, created_at, status unchanged in DB.
For any PAT with only workspaces:delete: list and get always return HTTP 404.
For any PAT with workspaces:read or workspaces:create: list/get return 200; mutation endpoints return 404 — unchanged from pre-feature baseline.

checks: All property test files compile: go build ./...
Property tests fail as expected (red) before implementation: go test ./internal/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSpec03_Group5'
No linter warnings introduced: go vet ./...
Test IDs TS-03-P1 through TS-03-P7 are all present

task_groups.title: Write tests: documentation existence and content (REQ-9, REQ-10)

subtasks.title: Write unit tests: docs/api.md covers all workspace endpoints and PATCH details
details: Open docs/api.md and assert presence of sections for all 7 workspace endpoints.
Assert docs/api.md contains 'PATCH /api/v1/workspaces', '128', '1024', 'archived', 'partial' or 'omitted fields', '400', '403', '404'.
Use Go file I/O (os.ReadFile) in test; assert string containment.
Test must fail if docs/api.md does not exist.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests: docs/api.md covers new scopes and non-workspace apikit endpoints
details: Assert docs/api.md contains 'workspaces:write', 'workspaces:delete', and implied read access documentation.
Assert docs/api.md contains sections for login, user, keys, tokens, orgs, and admin endpoints.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests: docs/cli.md covers all workspace subcommands and new update flags
details: Open docs/cli.md and assert presence of 'afc workspace create/list/get/update/archive/reactivate/delete'.
Assert docs/cli.md contains '--display-name', '--description', '--org', '--clear-display-name', '--clear-description', '--clear-org', exit code 1 behavior.
Test must fail if docs/cli.md does not exist.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests: docs/cli.md covers apikit-provided afc commands
details: Assert docs/cli.md contains sections for afc login, afc user, afc keys, afc tokens, afc orgs, afc admin.
String containment check (case-insensitive) using Go testing.

checks: All documentation test files compile: go build ./...
Documentation tests fail as expected (red) before docs are written: go test ./internal/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSpec03_Group6'
No linter warnings introduced: go vet ./...
Test IDs TS-03-35 through TS-03-41 are all present

task_groups.title: Implement workspace schema changes: DDL update and model struct

subtasks.title: Update workspaces table DDL to add display_name and description columns
details: Locate the CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces DDL (pre-production; no migration framework).
Add 'display_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ""' and 'description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ""' columns.
No ALTER TABLE logic — recreate schema for pre-production databases.
Ensure DDL matches the spec exactly including column order.

subtasks.title: Update Workspace Go struct and JSON serializer to include display_name and description
details: Add DisplayName string and Description string fields to the Workspace struct (internal/workspace/workspace.go or equivalent).
Add json tags: `json:"display_name"` and `json:"description"`.
Ensure fields are always serialized as strings (never null/omitempty).
Update all SQL SELECT queries that scan workspace rows to include new columns.

subtasks.title: Implement server-side normalization of display_name and description
details: Write normalizeDisplayName(slug, input *string) string: returns slug if input is nil or empty, else input value.
Write normalizeDescription(input *string) string: returns '' if input is nil, else input value.
Functions are called in both creation and update handlers before any DB write.
Use pointer semantics (*string) for optional JSON fields to distinguish null from absent.

checks: Schema tests pass: go test ./internal/workspace/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSpec03_Group1'
03-REQ-1.1, 03-REQ-1.2, 03-REQ-1.3, 03-REQ-1.4 acceptance criteria met
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1

task_groups.title: Implement new permission scopes and access control enforcement

subtasks.title: Register workspaces:write and workspaces:delete scopes via apikit.Permission in MountHandlers
details: Locate MountHandlers in the af-hub codebase.
Add apikit.Permission("workspaces:write", ...) registration alongside existing workspaces:read and workspaces:create.
Add apikit.Permission("workspaces:delete", ...) registration.
Verify exact apikit.Permission signature against the installed library version before implementation.
Do not alter existing workspaces:read or workspaces:create registrations.

subtasks.title: Implement workspaces:write implied read access in auth middleware
details: Update auth middleware/permission check: PAT with workspaces:write is permitted on list and get endpoints (same as workspaces:read).
PAT with only workspaces:delete: list and get endpoints return HTTP 404 (anti-enumeration) — no implied read.
Ensure scope-check helper hasReadAccess(pat) returns true for workspaces:read, workspaces:create, workspaces:write but false for workspaces:delete-only.

subtasks.title: Update endpoint auth guards for update, archive, reactivate, delete per access control matrix
details: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug: allow admin, owner API key, PAT workspaces:write (own). Deny all others with HTTP 404.
Archive/reactivate: same rule as PATCH.
DELETE: allow admin, owner API key, PAT workspaces:delete (own). Deny PAT workspaces:write with HTTP 404.
POST /api/v1/workspaces: deny admin token with HTTP 403.
Anti-enumeration: non-owner, non-admin → HTTP 404 regardless of resource existence.

checks: Permission scope tests pass: go test ./internal/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSpec03_Group2'
03-REQ-2.1 through 03-REQ-2.5, 03-REQ-3.1 through 03-REQ-3.7 acceptance criteria met
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1

subtasks.title: Implement workspace update handler: partial update, validation, and DB write
details: Create internal/workspace/update.go (or equivalent): func UpdateWorkspaceHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request).
Decode JSON body into a struct with pointer fields (*string DisplayName, *string Description, *string OrgID) to distinguish null from absent.
Reject immutable fields (slug, git_url, branch, owner_id) with HTTP 400 if present in body.
Apply normalizeDisplayName and normalizeDescription before DB write.
Build partial SQL UPDATE using only provided fields; set updated_at = now().
Return HTTP 200 with full updated workspace JSON.

subtasks.title: Implement update endpoint error handling: empty body, archived workspace, field length, org membership
details: Empty body (no fields provided): HTTP 400 before any DB read.
Archived workspace: HTTP 400 after loading workspace from DB.
display_name > 128 chars: HTTP 400, no DB write.
description > 1024 chars: HTTP 400, no DB write.
org_id non-null: call org membership service; if not member → HTTP 403; if service error/timeout → HTTP 500 with no partial write.
Use apikit's standard JSON error envelope for all error responses.

subtasks.title: Register PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug route in MountHandlers
details: Add route registration for PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug in MountHandlers.
Wire the route to UpdateWorkspaceHandler with the correct auth middleware chain.
Confirm route does not conflict with existing workspace routes.
Verify slug parameter extraction matches the existing routing convention (e.g., chi, gorilla/mux, or stdlib).

checks: Update endpoint tests pass: go test ./internal/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSpec03_Group3'
03-REQ-4.1 through 03-REQ-4.7 and all edge cases (03-REQ-4.E1 through 03-REQ-4.E8) acceptance criteria met
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1

task_groups.title: Update workspace creation handler to accept display_name and description

subtasks.title: Update POST /api/v1/workspaces request parsing to accept display_name and description
details: Update workspace creation request struct to include optional *string DisplayName and *string Description fields.
Apply normalizeDisplayName(slug, input) and normalizeDescription(input) before DB insert.
Apply length validation: display_name <= 128 chars, description <= 1024 chars → HTTP 400 if exceeded.
Existing requests without these fields must continue to return HTTP 201 with defaults.

subtasks.title: Update workspace creation SQL INSERT to persist display_name and description
details: Modify the INSERT INTO workspaces statement to include display_name and description columns.
Pass normalized values to the SQL statement parameters.
Confirm all existing workspace creation tests still pass after the change.

checks: Creation handler tests pass: go test ./internal/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSpec03_Group1|TestSpec03_Group6'
03-REQ-6.1, 03-REQ-6.2, 03-REQ-1.5, 03-REQ-1.6 acceptance criteria met
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1

task_groups.title: Implement afc workspace update CLI command and update afc workspace create flags

subtasks.title: Implement afc workspace update subcommand with all flags
details: Create cmd/workspace_update.go (or equivalent) implementing 'afc workspace update <slug>'.
Flags: --display-name string, --description string, --org string, --clear-display-name bool, --clear-description bool, --clear-org bool.
Validate: if no flags provided → print usage hint to stderr, exit 1, no HTTP call.
Build PATCH body including only explicitly-set flags; --clear-* flags send null values.
On success: print updated workspace JSON to stdout, exit 0.
Use pointer fields to track which flags were explicitly provided vs. absent.

subtasks.title: Implement afc workspace update error handling: API errors, timeout, malformed response
details: Non-2xx HTTP response: extract error field from JSON body, print to stderr, exit 1.
Network timeout or connection failure: print timeout/connection error to stderr, exit 1 within configured client timeout.
HTTP 200 with malformed/missing-field response body: print parse error description to stderr, exit 1.
Use configured HTTP client timeout (context deadline); do not hang indefinitely.

subtasks.title: Add --display-name and --description flags to afc workspace create
details: Add optional --display-name and --description flags to the existing afc workspace create command.
When provided, include in POST /api/v1/workspaces request body.
When omitted, do not include in request body (server applies defaults).
All existing workspace create flags and behavior remain unchanged.

task_groups.title: Create docs/api.md and docs/cli.md reference documentation

subtasks.title: Write docs/api.md: workspace endpoints, new PATCH endpoint, permission scopes
details: Create docs/api.md covering all 7 workspace endpoints: create, list, get, update, archive, reactivate, delete.
For each endpoint: authentication requirements, request body schema, response schema, permission scope requirements, HTTP error codes.
PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug: document partial-update semantics, display_name max 128 chars, description max 1024 chars, all error conditions (400, 403, 404, 500).
Document workspaces:write and workspaces:delete scopes, their implied permissions, and which endpoints they authorize.

subtasks.title: Extend docs/api.md: non-workspace apikit endpoints

subtasks.title: Write docs/cli.md: all afc workspace subcommands and afc workspace update flags
details: Create docs/cli.md covering all workspace subcommands: create, list, get, update, archive, reactivate, delete.
For each subcommand: all flags, argument formats, and exit codes.
afc workspace update: document --display-name, --description, --org, --clear-display-name, --clear-description, --clear-org and exit-1 behavior when no flags provided.

subtasks.title: Extend docs/cli.md: apikit-provided afc commands
details: Add sections for afc login, afc user, afc keys, afc tokens, afc orgs, afc admin.
Each section at the same level of detail as workspace commands: flags, argument formats, exit codes.

task_groups.title: Wiring verification: end-to-end integration, stub audit, and smoke tests

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths: verify each path's entry point calls the next function in the chain
details: 03-PATH-1: Confirm afc workspace update → PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug → auth middleware → UpdateWorkspaceHandler → DB write. Each function in the chain calls the next — no stubs.
03-PATH-2: Confirm DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug → auth middleware (workspaces:delete check) → delete handler → DB row removal.
03-PATH-3: Confirm afc workspace create --display-name → POST /api/v1/workspaces → creation handler with normalization → DB insert.
03-PATH-4: Confirm PATCH on archived workspace → update handler → HTTP 400 returned, no DB write.

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation: callers receive and use return values from update handler and auth middleware
details: Confirm UpdateWorkspaceHandler returns the full workspace struct to the HTTP response writer (not just a status code).
Confirm auth middleware's permission-check return value is used to gate handler invocation — not silently discarded.
Confirm org membership check result is used to set HTTP 403 vs. proceed with DB write — error is not swallowed.
Confirm updated_at from DB is included in the response (not the pre-update value).

subtasks.title: Run smoke tests with all real components
details: TS-03-SMOKE-1: PAT workspaces:write updates display_name via afc CLI → HTTP 200, DB updated, afc exits 0.
TS-03-SMOKE-2: PAT workspaces:delete deletes archived workspace by slug → HTTP 200, row removed from DB.
TS-03-SMOKE-3: afc workspace create --display-name --description → HTTP 201, both fields in response.
TS-03-SMOKE-4: PATCH on archived workspace with valid owner API key → HTTP 400, no DB changes.
Run with go test ./... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSmoke03' using real server, real DB, real afc binary.

subtasks.title: Stub and dead-code audit: search for panic("not implemented"), bare returns, and TODO markers
details: Search codebase for Go stub markers: panic("not implemented"), // TODO, bare 'return nil' in non-trivial handler paths.
Run: grep -rn 'panic("not implemented")\|// TODO\|// FIXME' internal/ cmd/ to surface unfinished code.
Confirm UpdateWorkspaceHandler, normalizeDisplayName, normalizeDescription, and all new CLI commands are fully implemented with no stubs.
Confirm the PATCH route is registered in MountHandlers — not just defined as a handler function.

subtasks.title: Cross-spec entry point verification: confirm upstream workspace infrastructure wires into new handlers
details: Verify that MountHandlers (from spec 01_workspaces infrastructure) includes the PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug route alongside existing routes.
Verify apikit.Permission registrations for workspaces:write and workspaces:delete are called within MountHandlers (not dead code).
Verify afc workspace update command is registered in the root CLI command group (not just defined in isolation).
Run go test ./... -count=1 -v and confirm no test references a nil handler or unregistered route.

subtasks.title: Call-site verification: confirm new functions are called from production code, not just tested
details: Confirm normalizeDisplayName is called from both the creation handler and the update handler in production code paths.
Confirm normalizeDescription is called from both the creation handler and the update handler in production code paths.
Confirm UpdateWorkspaceHandler is wired as the HTTP handler for PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug in production (MountHandlers), not only in tests.
Use grep or Go AST analysis to verify call sites exist in non-test files: grep -n 'normalizeDisplayName\|normalizeDescription\|UpdateWorkspaceHandler' internal/ cmd/ (excluding _test.go files).

All spec tests pass: go test ./... -count=1
